Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Normal Process Flow: 1. Register Order (Sales) 2. Perform Credit Check (Finance) 3. Validate Stock (Warehouse) 4. Confirm Shipment (Logistics) 5. Ship Goods (Logistics) 6. Issue Invoice (Finance) 7. Receive Payment (Finance) Now let me look at each case: Case 1001 – Follows the normal flow perfectly (steps 1-7 in order). No anomalies.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1002 – Activities in order: 1. Register Order (event 8, 08:20) 2. Confirm Shipment (event 9, 08:25) SKIPPED Credit Check and Validate Stock; shipped BEFORE credit check 3. Ship Goods (event 10, 08:40) Shipped before credit check 4. Perform Credit Check (event 11, 09:10) Credit check AFTER shipment 5. Validate Stock (event 12, 09:15) Stock validation AFTER shipment 6. Issue Invoice (event 13, 09:30) 7. Receive Payment (event 14, 10:00 next day) Anomalies: - Credit Check and Validate Stock happen AFTER Ship Goods (out of order / skipped in proper sequence) - attempted_early=Y on Confirm Shipment suggests someone tried to rush the process

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1003 – Activities in order: 1. Register Order (event 15, 08:45) 2. Perform Credit Check (event 16, 09:00) 3. Ship Goods (event 17, 09:10) SKIPPED Validate Stock and Confirm Shipment 4. Issue Invoice (event 18, 09:30) 5. Confirm Shipment (event 19, 09:45) late_confirmation=Y, confirmed AFTER goods shipped 6. Receive Payment (event 20, 11:00) Anomalies: - Validate Stock was skipped entirely - Confirm Shipment happens after Ship Goods (reverse order) - late_confirmation=Y confirms the anomaly - Confirm Shipment and Validate Stock are both missing from the proper sequence

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1004 – Activities in order: 1. Register Order (event 21, 09:00) 2. Receive Payment (event 22, 09:05) Payment received immediately after registration, before any processing 3. Issue Invoice (event 23, 09:20) Invoice AFTER payment 4. Confirm Shipment (event 24, 09:25) shipment_scheduled=N 5. Ship Goods (event 25, 09:50) Anomalies: - Receive Payment happens right after Register Order (within 5 minutes!) — before any credit check, stock validation, or shipment - Credit Check and Validate Stock are entirely missing - Issue Invoice comes AFTER Receive Payment - shipment_scheduled=N on Confirm Shipment - No payment received event... wait, it has Receive Payment but no prior credit check

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Let me now formulate SQL queries to investigate these. Let me think about comprehensive SQL queries: 1. Detect cases where activities are out of the expected order — e.g., Ship Goods before Credit Check or Validate Stock 2. Detect cases missing key activities (Credit Check, Validate Stock) 3. Detect cases where Receive Payment happens unusually early (before Ship Goods) 4. Find resources that are involved in anomalous cases 5. Check order values for anomalous cases — are higher-value orders more prone to bypassing controls? 6. Find activities that happen out of sequence 7. Detect unusual timing patterns
